feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Handling large file transfers (1GB+) in BTP-Integration Suite !
entry.content:
We are currently evaluating the migration of high-volume 'File-to-File' pass-through scenarios (exceeding 1GB) from SAP PI/PO to SAP BTP Integration Suite. While this is currently stable in our legacy environment, we need to ensure the Cloud Integration architecture can handle similar loads without memory bottlenecks.
when we try the same requirement in CPI ,it’s getting failed to read the file .

Any recommendations would be appreciated,thank you

-regards ,Bhaskar

Cloud Integration

entry.title: Could not fetch access token from the XSUAA
entry.content:
Dear experts,

could you please give me a hint how could I solve one interesting problem? We have implemented pretty simple iFlow on Integration Suite, which is started using HTTP Adapter and for which we have generated Credentials in BTP... But I try to start this iFlow from 3 different environments and I could start this flow just only from 2 environments even we are using the same environments...

In the traces for a call which we are not able to process I see these logs:

com.sap.it.security.multicloud.authentication.ResponseErrorHandlerImpl#anonymous#it-sap-servlet-container-16...Could not fetch access token from the XSUAA: HTTP 400 BAD_REQUEST Bad Request:...

com.sap.it.security.multicloud.authentication.UaaTokenBroker#anonymous#it-sap-servlet-container-16...com.sap.it.security.multicloud.authentication#na#na#na#na#The client certificate is not configured in a service key of a Process Integration Runtime service instance. Client certificate: ...

As I mention, I'm using the same credentials from all the environments and in 2 cases it is process and in 1 case I get this error...

BR,

Milan

entry.title: Connection to IXOS Server from CPI - Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My requirement is to store the PDF files into IXOS server which is an opentext server for archiving the files and once we send any file we will get a archive id and it should be updated in S4HANA . Could anyone provide inputs on how to establish connection to IXOS server i mean how to store files in IXOS server from CPI whether it is API based push or SFTP based push. 

 

Regards,

Pavan Kalyan Racha

entry.title: Pre-Exit and Post-Exit Extensions in Standard Integration Flows
entry.content:
Pre-Exit and Post-Exit Extensions in Standard Integration Flows

Pre-Exit and Post-Exit Extensions in standard packages allow you to implement additional requirements within the Integration Flow without changing its status to “Modified.”

It is important to preserve standard packages because:

  • You can receive updates released by the vendor.
  • Triggers do not consume the message quota.

 

Now, let’s examine how to activate a Post-Exit extension through a simple scenario.

 

To activate the extension, you need to configure a predefined Exchange Property value to “true”

Pre-Exit runs before the mapping or before a specific processing step, depending on the extension point defined within the standard iFlow.

Post-Exit is triggered at a specific point within the standard iFlow (usually after the mapping). In some SAP packages can be used to access both the original and transformed payload.

Note: The custom IFlow created for Pre-Exit or Post-Exit may include a Receiver Adapters depending on your requirements.

For the Post-Exit, you define the path of the Receiver Process Direct adapter configured in the standard flow within the Sender Process Direct adapter of your custom flow.

After performing the required processing in the custom flow, you must transform the payload into the format expected by the standard flow.

In the standard flow, there is a filter step after the Receiver Process Direct step. The payload returned from the custom Post-Exit flow must be structured in a way that it can pass through this filter.

By following these steps, you can implement additional requirements in standard processes without modifying the original flow.

entry.title: Client Certificate Authentication change for Inbound communication
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I have notification from SAP about Client Certificate Authentication change for Inbound communication.
What i have done is check from Integration Suite on Manage Security >> Keystore. CMIIW

Whether any other workaround should I check?

"
SAP understands the critical importance of ensuring constant availability of your Integration scenarios. Based on the recent announcement made by the Certification Authorities (CAs) we would like to assure you that SAP remains committed to ensure stability and trust with SAP Integration Suite. 
Certification Authorities (CAs) are removing the Client Authentication (ClientAuth) Extended Key Usage (EKU) from publicly trusted SSL/TLS certificates. Public CAs began phasing it out by default in September 2025, and some CAs may stop including it in newly issued public certificates as early as May 2026, when broader removal is expected across the industry.

This change affects inbound scenarios where external systems authenticate to SAP Integration Suite using client certificates issued by public certificate authorities. 

Impact 

If your inbound integrations use publicly issued client certificates, renewed certificates after May 2026 may no longer contain ClientAuth EKU. 
This can lead to TLS handshake failures and disruption of inbound connectivity. 

For further details, please refer to the related SAP Note: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003711618 

Action Required 

To avoid disruption, please review your inbound mTLS setup and take one of the following actions as a part of your next certificate renewal: 

Recommended: Use SAP Cloud Root CA–issued client certificates via the Destination Service . 
Alternative: Configure a Custom Domain and manage your own certificate trust setup"


Regards,

entry.title: SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Integration Suit - Segregation of Duties Role Matrix
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community Experts, While reviewing SAP Note 2782388, I was unable to find a standard cloud Segregation of Duties (SoD) ruleset covering SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry) and SAP Integration Suite

We are currently designing a manual SoD matrix and would like to align with SAP recommended best practices.

Could you please help with one or more of the following

1. Is there any official SAP standard SoD ruleset (or reference content) available for:

  • SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry) platform role collections (Global Account / Directory / Subaccount SAP Integration Suite role collections (Cloud Integration, API Management, Grap. 

2. If no standard ruleset exists, could you share recommended SoD guidance, such as:

  • A reference document, blog, or SAP Help page
  • A list of high risk role combinations (top 10–15) that should not be assigned to the same user, for example:
  • Between BTP platform role collections
  • Between SAP Integration Suite role collections
  • Between BTP platform roles and Integration Suite roles

3. From your experience, which role collections are considered “critical” or “admin level” and should generally be segregated (for example: tenant admin vs developer, transport vs development, configuration vs monitoring)?

Any guidance, references, or practical experience would be highly helpful

Thanks

Raj

entry.title: Mail Sender Adapter For Microsoft 365
entry.content:
If you need to create a mail sender adapter for a mail address which is based on Microsoft 365, the step by step explanation is below.

First you need to understand that microsoft 365 use their authorization method as Oauth2 Authorization Code, so for you to define that you first need to ask the mail owner/admin to share the following informations  with you:

Mail Address, Password, client id, client secret, auth endpoint, token endpoint.

In addition to that in microsoft azure portal below parameters should be given

https://outlook.office.com/IMAP.AccessAsUser.All

https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send

Here is a screen shot of azure portal.

So after that you need to define this oauth2 authorization code in security material with the credentials you get.

Here is a screenshot of it.

 

When you deploy this, you will see it as unauthorized just like below.

It is not problem at all, you should click on three dot and click on authorize afterwards. It will open the mail login page in a new tab.

You can simply put e mail adress and password credentials and login, this will send an approval to the portal and you should ask them to approve. 

After that status of oauth2 authorization code will convert to deployed it means you can go to next step which is creating the sender mail adapter for getting the mails.

After that step you can basically go to your flow and create a mail sender adapter.

You should note that microsoft 365 use IMAPS so you should choose IMAPS.

Host and Port informations are as follows outlook.office365.com:993

In the processing tab you basically define what want to do with the e-mail, which folder you check and etc.

And finally there is a scheduler tab that you define pulling interval.

This way you finish your work with mail side of the flow. You can map the data inside that mail, data store send it to some other mail or you can use it as you need.

entry.title: Provide values for node created with CreateIf in CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

I've some rather confusing issue in CPI.

I'm creating a node "ContactComms" via createIf based on some conditions. I have 7 values in the queue for the createIf, one is "true" and is resulting in a node as expected.

Within node "ContactComms", there is a tag "Value". Although number of values provided in the queue for this is the same, I never get a value in "Value".

The result has no tag "value". I would expect one with mail ab-email@gmail.com

I am happy for any hints on this issues. Thank you very much!

Harry

 

EDIT: As I wrote, I came to the conclusion. For value, "splitByValue" is the right function to use instead of "removeContext"

entry.title: CPI SOAP error - HTTP response '400: Bad Request'
entry.content:
Hi,

When an external SOAP service is called via SOAP UI with proper request payload, we get expected response, but when we use the same request payload to call the same Service via SAP CPI, I get the below error

org.apache.cxf.interceptor.Fault: Could not send Message., cause: org.apache.cxf.transport.http.HTTPException: HTTP response '400: Bad Request. Bad Request' when communicating with URL

It was working fine until 06/03/2026. The SOAP vendor doesn't update anything, so I suspect SAP has changed something.

Has anyone got this error before?

Regards,

Tri

entry.title: ProcessDirect Header Propagation Issue – BP_NUMBER Not Available in Called iFlows
entry.content:
Hello SAP Cloud Integration experts,

I’m working on an integration scenario where I’ve decomposed a large iFlow into multiple smaller iFlows using the ProcessDirect adapter. The main iFlow calls three separate iFlows in parallel:

Billing
BUPA
Accounting

Requirement

I need to pass a bp_number (Business Partner Number) from the incoming HTTP request to all called iFlows dynamically.

What I implemented

Extracted bp_number from query parameters using a Groovy script

Stored it as:
Exchange Property → BP_NUMBER
Message Header → BP_NUMBER

Before calling ProcessDirect, I ensured:

Header: BP_NUMBER = ${property.BP_NUMBER}

In the called iFlows, I use:

${header.BP_NUMBER} inside HTTP receiver queries.
I even added BP_NUMBER in the allowed headers for all the child flows inside the Integration Process Runtime Configuration

Issue

In all called iFlows, BP_NUMBER is coming as empty (''), causing API failures:


Is using headers the recommended way to pass dynamic values across iFlows?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Is it possible to connect two XI adapters to the same SAP backend (PI and Integration Suite)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently evaluating a migration from SAP PI/PO to the SAP Integration Suite.

In our current setup, several interfaces use ABAP proxies via the XI adapter.

During the transition phase we are wondering:

  • Is it technically possible to connect two XI adapter endpoints to the same SAP backend system as sender and receiver?
    • one pointing to PI/PO
    • one pointing to Integration Suite

Questions:

  1. Can an SAP backend system maintain two XI adapter connections simultaneously?
  2. How would the proxy decide which endpoint to use (logical port, configuration, etc.)?
  3. Are there any best practices or limitations for this setup during migration?

Any experiences or recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: How to Rewrite REST-style Paths to OData-style Paths in SAP Integration Suite API Management?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working with SAP Integration Suite – API Management and I am trying to implement a routing transformation where an incoming REST-style resource path (e.g. /customer/12345) should be rewritten into an OData-style entity key format (e.g. /Customer('12345')) before the call is forwarded to the backend system.

✅ What I am trying to achieve

Incoming request:

/customer/12345

Backend should receive:

/customer('12345')

The backend system is configured through an API Provider, which already defines:

  • Hostname
  • Port
  • Base Path
  • Location ID
  • Authentication (Basic Auth)
  • On-Premise connectivity (Cloud Connector)

So ideally, I would like to reuse all these settings and only rewrite the path suffix.

✅ What I have already tried

1. Using AssignMessage in TargetEndpoint PreFlow

I attempted to set a new <Path> in the TargetEndpoint:

 
<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Set>
	    <Verb>GET</Verb>
	    <Path>/customer('12345')</Path>
	</Set>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<AssignTo createNew="true" transport="http" type="request" />
</AssignMessage>

I have also tested it on the ProxyEndpoint. I have also set the target.copy.pathsuffixto false.

Result:
The backend still receives the original API Provider base path, and the rewritten path is ignored.

2. Using AssignMessage in TargetEndpoint PostFlow

3. Setting target.url dynamically

I tested rewriting the full backend URL in JavaScript or AssignMessage to bypass the API Provider:

 
<AssignVariable>
    <Name>target.url</Name>
    <Value>https://host:port/Customer('12345')</Value>
</AssignVariable>

Result:
This technically rewrites the path, but it breaks the API Provider setup:

  • Basic Authentication is no longer applied
  • Cloud Connector / On-Premise routing does not work
  • Any certificates / trust settings from the API Provider are lost
  • I receive HTTP errors such as ServiceUnavailable or BadGateway

So this option is not suitable.

Only setting a relative path /customer('12345')ignores the suffix.

4. Rewriting request.path or proxy.pathsuffix

Based on Apigee documentation, these would be ideal, but in SAP Integration Suite:

  • request.path is read-only
  • proxy.pathsuffix is read-only
  • target.basepath is read-only
  • Only target.copy.pathsuffix is writable, but it only controls whether the suffix is appended, not what it contains

So these variables cannot be used to rewrite the path.

5. Using servicecallout.requesturi

I learned that this variable only applies to ServiceCallout policies, not to the main backend request.
Therefore it cannot be used for rewriting the TargetEndpoint URI.

✅ Where I am stuck

At this point, I have not found a reliable way to:

  • Rewrite only the resource path (REST → OData),
  • While keeping all API Provider settings intact
  • And without manually setting target.url (which overrides everything)

It seems that SAP API Management does not allow rewriting the backend path when an API Provider is used, unless the path is completely removed.

✅ My question to the community

Is there a supported way in SAP Integration Suite API Management to rewrite only the backend path (e.g. for REST → OData transformation) while still using the API Provider configuration for host, base path, authentication, and connectivity?

Specifically:

  • Where would the rewriting need to happen (ProxyEndpoint vs TargetEndpoint & Pre-Flow vs. Post-Flow)?
  • Is there a recommended approach or best practice for converting REST-style paths to OData paths in API Management?
  • Or is this transformation simply not possible when using API Providers?

Any guidance, examples, or clarification would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Strengthen Kafka integration
entry.content:
Hello community!

For an international customer, I’m looking for insights to strengthen the integration with Kafka, in an EDA context.

I’m specifically looking for these topics

  • Private Link Architecture. B2B VPN tunnel, Cloud Connector, … ?
  • Multiple event types on a single topic
  • Better visibility on errors, especially when multiple schema rules are violated
  • Offset commit mechanism
  • AVRO schema support
  • Retry behavior and flexible strategy

Yes, I know there is Advanced Event Mesh. But the choice for Kafka was made a long time ago. Currently, we do not use the native Kafka adapter within the Integration Suite.

I’m thus looking for insights within the Integration Suite context to strengthen our current integration. To make it more robust, safe and futureproof.

Looking forward to your feedback!

Thanks.
Dimitri

entry.title: Cannot see the toggle button to enable anomaly detection in Integration suite
entry.content:
Cannot see the toggle button to enable anomaly detection in Integration suite.

 

AS PER THE NOTE , REGION is supported and Integration suit is upgraded to enhanced edition. But still cannot see the toggle button to enable Anomaly detection

 

Integration Suite

entry.title: Integration btw SAP SuccessFactors to SAP Ariba for user master data flow ..
entry.content:
need to Integrate  Success factors to SAP Ariba for  User master data integration as if the user is created in SF that can automatically be created in Ariba....what architecture we are thinking is  

Success Factors->SFTP->CPI->CIG->Ariba Buying/Sourcing.

Can anyone suggest will this work or not and if there is any other approach for the same requirement. and what actions mapping will be required from CIG, Ariba end for the requires integration.

entry.title: How to Create Custom DRF Outbound Implementation for Table T880 in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on Data Replication Framework (DRF) in SAP S/4HANA and have a requirement to replicate data from table T880 (Company master data) to an external system.

As per my understanding, T880 is not available as a standard DRF-supported business object.

I would like to understand the correct approach for this scenario:

  1. How can we define a custom DRF business object for table T880?

  2. What are the steps to create a custom outbound implementation (Z*)?

  3. How to implement and link the outbound ABAP class with DRF?

  4. How is replication triggered for such custom objects (change pointers or manual DRFOUT)?

  5. In real projects, is DRF recommended for configuration tables like T880, or is a custom interface a better approach?

Any guidance, documentation, or sample implementation would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Unable to set property from an XML using the XPath.
entry.content:
Hello Experts.

I am unable to set a property using an XPATH from the XML Payload.

Following is the XML payload.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<entry xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"
xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"
xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices"
xml:base="https://api41preview.sapsf.com/odata/v2/">
<content type="application/xml">hello</content>
<d:fileExtension>txt</d:fileExtension>
</entry>

Note that I have simplified this payload eliminating all the other nodes so as to focus on the question at hand. 

Following is the sample I-flow I created to explain the issue.

In Content Modifier 1 I have hardcoded the body with the Payload

In Content Modifier 2 I am setting the Property using the Xpath as follows

Action Name Type Data Type Value

ActionNameTypeData TypeValue

Create

fileExtension

XPath

java.lang.String

/entry/d:fileExtension/text()

In Content Modifier 3 I am checking if the Property is set. This is where I find that the property is not getting set.

I have confirmed that the XPATH is correct testing it wit other tools.

I have also included the namespaces d and m on the Runtime Configuration Tab on the Integration Flow as follows

entry.title: Migrating SAP CPI Neo to SAP Integration Suite, do we need to convert Groovy Script to JavaScript ?
entry.content:
While migrating SAP CPI Neo to SAP Integration Suite, do we need to convert Groovy Script to JavaScript ?

Is there any official documentation or NOTE available for the information ?

entry.title: Issue with Monitoring SAP Event Mesh in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hallo Everyone, 
I would like to monitor SAP Event Mesh in Cloud ALM. Currently, I am able to see all the Event Mesh systems that we have subscribed to in SAP BTP under Landscape Management in Cloud ALM.

However, when I attempt to add an endpoint for Event Mesh, I receive the following message:
“The endpoint creation is not required for SAP Event Mesh, as none of the SAP Cloud ALM use cases requires an endpoint.”

 

In the Health Monitoring section, when I add the service to scope, there is no option to proceed further, and no additional information related to Event Mesh is displayed.

I have already enabled data collection, but for over a month now, no data has been collected. The system shows the following error message:
“Data is outdated. Data collection turned OFF.”


Could you please assist in understanding:

  • Whether SAP Event Mesh monitoring is fully supported in Cloud ALM
  • If any additional configuration steps are required to enable data collection
  • Why the data collection remains turned off despite being enabled

I would appreciate any guidance or suggestions you can provide.

Thank you in Advance. 

Kind regards, 
Venkata Nagendra Reddy Vardhireddy 

 

SAP Cloud ALM SAP Event Mesh SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: SAP IA MAG Stop producing empty elements
entry.content:
I'm working on a MAG that maps an XML idoc to an EDIFACT INVOIC message. I'm now stuck on an issue where I always get an empty element generated when no element should be produced.
See the screenshot for the first element

The second element never is produced with a very similar function and condition

How can I stop the element from being produced?

SAP Integration Suite
